categories.title: Recent Issues and Fixes in ElizaOS CLI and Twitter Integration

content.text: A fix was implemented to resolve a Twitter API error (code 187) caused by sending duplicate tweets consecutively. This issue was tracked in GitHub issue #4086 and resolved in pull request #4111. The fix ensures that identical tweet content is not sent back-to-back, which previously triggered the duplicate status error.

Users are experiencing installation failures when attempting to install @elizaos/cli via npm due to a missing dependency version (@elizaos/plugin-sql@^0.25.6). The npm registry does not contain this version, only pre-releases for 1.0.0. This results in an ETARGET error during installation. The issue suggests that the package.json for @elizaos/cli should be updated to reference a valid version of the plugin.

A bug was reported where running `npx elizaos create` followed by `npx elizaos start` results in an error stating 'agents already exists'. This occurs on Node.js v23.7.0 and Debian 12. The issue appears during the initialization of a new agent project using the ElizaOS CLI.
A new 'monorepo' command was added to the CLI, enhancing its functionality and supporting monorepo workflows.

Recent development efforts focused on package improvements, including Twitter plugin post generation and running a linter. Two bugs were fixed, and four pull requests were merged by five contributors. The addition of the monorepo command was a key feature in this update.

categories.title: Plugin Fixes and Enhancements for Twitter and Telegram Integrations

content.text: The Twitter plugin has been updated to improve post generation reliability. A new check prevents tweets containing 'Error:' from being posted, and the post generation logic has been corrected by replacing the previous method with `composePromptFromState`. Additionally, code formatting was improved by running a linter.

The Telegram plugin received a comprehensive update to enhance functionality and maintainability. Key improvements include standardized world ID creation using chat IDs, alignment of message event structures with the core system, and the addition of a `startTelegramOnboarding` function for better onboarding in group chats. Entity handling was improved with better chat owner identification and standardized entity representation. The codebase was refactored for clarity, including separation of entity building and reusable chat type helpers. Known limitations include incomplete support for forums, topics, and channels, as well as issues with entity tracking and deprecated event handlers. Future work will focus on refining these areas.
The current price of Wrapped Bitcoin (WBTC) stands at $82,581.85, reflecting its strong position in the crypto market as a tokenized version of Bitcoin on the Ethereum blockchain.
The token ai16z is currently priced at $0.1579, indicating a significantly lower market value compared to major cryptocurrencies like WBTC.

categories.title: ElizaOS Development Updates: CLI Enhancements, Plugin Fixes, and Installation Issues

content.text: A new CLI feature was introduced with the addition of a 'monorepo' command, enhancing developer tooling for managing multi-package repositories.

The Twitter plugin received multiple fixes: one addressing duplicate tweet errors (Twitter error 187) by preventing consecutive identical tweets, and another improving tweet generation by checking for error messages before posting and refactoring the post generation logic.
Component handling was improved to avoid unnecessary data fetching when components are not mounted, optimizing performance and resource usage.

Significant updates were made to the Telegram plugin, including standardized world ID creation, improved message event structure, onboarding support, and better entity handling. Future improvements are planned for forum/topic support, channel integration, and entity tracking.

Users reported installation failures due to a missing dependency version (@elizaos/plugin-sql@^0.25.6). Investigation revealed that only pre-release versions exist. The quickstart guide was also found to be outdated, and users must use the @beta tag to install successfully.
A bug was reported where running `npx elizaos create` followed by `npx elizaos start` results in an 'agents already exists' error. This issue affects users on Node.js v23.7.0 and Debian 12.
